Prefer high reversibility and explicit lexical preservation.
Use enough context for BM25, but avoid long explanatory sentences.

- Do not output bullet points, explanations, or JSON.
- Do not invent facts.

retrieved_titles: I Think You Think Too Much of Me
Oh, the Thinks You Can Think!
retrieved_docs: Six Thinking Hats | Six Thinking Hats is a system designed by Edward de Bono which describes a tool for group discussion and individual thinking involving six colored hats.  "Six Thinking Hats" and the associated idea parallel thinking provide a means for groups to plan thinking processes in a detailed and cohesive way, and in doing so to think together more effectively.

I Think You Think Too Much of Me | I Think You Think Too Much of Me (stylised as i think you think too much of me) is the second EP by Irish musician EDEN, released on 19 August 2016 through his own label MCMXCV and distributed by Astralwerks.  The EP was recorded and produced in Dublin and contains seven tracks spanning approximately 26 minutes.  "i think you think too much of me" is the first of Ng's works to be released on physical media, as it was produced in two 12-inch vinyl formats, clear red and clear, in addition to Compact Cassette.

Oh, the Thinks You Can Think! | Oh, the Thinks You Can Think!  is a children's book written and illustrated by Theodor Geisel under the pen name Dr. Seuss and published by Random House on August 21, 1975.  The book is about the many amazing 'thinks' one can think and the endless possibilities and dreams that imagination can create.

Thinking and Destiny | "Thinking and Destiny" by Harold W. Percival on "The law of thought".  Its basic premise is that thinking creates karma or destiny.  The book shows how to perfect our thinking and learn how to think without creating thoughts.

Thinking outside the box | Thinking outside the box (also thinking out of the box or thinking beyond the box and, especially in Australia, thinking outside the square) is a metaphor that means to think differently, unconventionally, or from a new perspective.  This phrase often refers to novel or creative thinking.  The term is thought to derive from management consultants in the 1970s and 1980s challenging their clients to solve the "nine dots" puzzle, whose solution requires some lateral thinking.  This phrase can also be found commonly in dance, as encouragement to move creatively, beyond simple, geometric box steps and their basic variations, to literally step outside the box into more complex patterns of expression.

Speed thinking | Speed thinking is a type of thinking technique proposed by authors such as Dr Ken Hudson, Malcolm Gladwell and Edward De Bono that, according to the authors, could accelerate the pace of thinking and improve how an individual or group thinks, creates, solves and acts.
Visual thinking | Visual thinking, also called visual/spatial learning or picture thinking is the phenomenon of thinking through visual processing.

Key identifiers include the album title ("Emperors of Soul"), the artist ("The Temptations"), the label ("Motown Records"), and the format ("five-disc box set"). The compilation highlights The Temptations' legacy as pioneers of Motown's vocal group sound.

retrieved_docs: Six Thinking Hats | Six Thinking Hats is a system designed by Edward de Bono which describes a tool for group discussion and individual thinking involving six colored hats.  "Six Thinking Hats" and the associated idea parallel thinking provide a means for groups to plan thinking processes in a detailed and cohesive way, and in doing so to think together more effectively.

I Think You Think Too Much of Me | I Think You Think Too Much of Me (stylised as i think you think too much of me) is the second EP by Irish musician EDEN, released on 19 August 2016 through his own label MCMXCV and distributed by Astralwerks.  The EP was recorded and produced in Dublin and contains seven tracks spanning approximately 26 minutes.  "i think you think too much of me" is the first of Ng's works to be released on physical media, as it was produced in two 12-inch vinyl formats, clear red and clear, in addition to Compact Cassette.

Oh, the Thinks You Can Think! | Oh, the Thinks You Can Think!  is a children's book written and illustrated by Theodor Geisel under the pen name Dr. Seuss and published by Random House on August 21, 1975.  The book is about the many amazing 'thinks' one can think and the endless possibilities and dreams that imagination can create.

Thinking and Destiny | "Thinking and Destiny" by Harold W. Percival on "The law of thought".  Its basic premise is that thinking creates karma or destiny.  The book shows how to perfect our thinking and learn how to think without creating thoughts.

Thinking outside the box | Thinking outside the box (also thinking out of the box or thinking beyond the box and, especially in Australia, thinking outside the square) is a metaphor that means to think differently, unconventionally, or from a new perspective.  This phrase often refers to novel or creative thinking.  The term is thought to derive from management consultants in the 1970s and 1980s challenging their clients to solve the "nine dots" puzzle, whose solution requires some lateral thinking.  This phrase can also be found commonly in dance, as encouragement to move creatively, beyond simple, geometric box steps and their basic variations, to literally step outside the box into more complex patterns of expression.

Speed thinking | Speed thinking is a type of thinking technique proposed by authors such as Dr Ken Hudson, Malcolm Gladwell and Edward De Bono that, according to the authors, could accelerate the pace of thinking and improve how an individual or group thinks, creates, solves and acts.
Visual thinking | Visual thinking, also called visual/spatial learning or picture thinking is the phenomenon of thinking through visual processing.

"Emperors of Soul," a 1994 five-disc box set compilation released by Motown Records, is dedicated to **The Temptations**, an American vocal group and Motown artists. The compilation spans approximately four decades of their career, from their early work with the group *The Distants* (e.g., the 1959 recording *"Come On"*) through material up to 1994, including four new tracks by the then-current Temptations lineup. The lineup at the time of the compilation included **Ali-Ollie Woodson**, **Theo Peoples**, **Ron Tyson**, **Otis Williams**, and **Melvin Franklin**. - **Lineup**: Specific members of the Temptations as of the 1994 release.

Released by **Motown Records**, it serves as a career-spanning anthology highlighting their contributions to Motown's vocal harmony legacy.

retrieved_docs: Six Thinking Hats | Six Thinking Hats is a system designed by Edward de Bono which describes a tool for group discussion and individual thinking involving six colored hats.  "Six Thinking Hats" and the associated idea parallel thinking provide a means for groups to plan thinking processes in a detailed and cohesive way, and in doing so to think together more effectively.

I Think You Think Too Much of Me | I Think You Think Too Much of Me (stylised as i think you think too much of me) is the second EP by Irish musician EDEN, released on 19 August 2016 through his own label MCMXCV and distributed by Astralwerks.  The EP was recorded and produced in Dublin and contains seven tracks spanning approximately 26 minutes.  "i think you think too much of me" is the first of Ng's works to be released on physical media, as it was produced in two 12-inch vinyl formats, clear red and clear, in addition to Compact Cassette.

Oh, the Thinks You Can Think! | Oh, the Thinks You Can Think!  is a children's book written and illustrated by Theodor Geisel under the pen name Dr. Seuss and published by Random House on August 21, 1975.  The book is about the many amazing 'thinks' one can think and the endless possibilities and dreams that imagination can create.

Thinking and Destiny | "Thinking and Destiny" by Harold W. Percival on "The law of thought".  Its basic premise is that thinking creates karma or destiny.  The book shows how to perfect our thinking and learn how to think without creating thoughts.

Thinking outside the box | Thinking outside the box (also thinking out of the box or thinking beyond the box and, especially in Australia, thinking outside the square) is a metaphor that means to think differently, unconventionally, or from a new perspective.  This phrase often refers to novel or creative thinking.  The term is thought to derive from management consultants in the 1970s and 1980s challenging their clients to solve the "nine dots" puzzle, whose solution requires some lateral thinking.  This phrase can also be found commonly in dance, as encouragement to move creatively, beyond simple, geometric box steps and their basic variations, to literally step outside the box into more complex patterns of expression.

Speed thinking | Speed thinking is a type of thinking technique proposed by authors such as Dr Ken Hudson, Malcolm Gladwell and Edward De Bono that, according to the authors, could accelerate the pace of thinking and improve how an individual or group thinks, creates, solves and acts.
Visual thinking | Visual thinking, also called visual/spatial learning or picture thinking is the phenomenon of thinking through visual processing.

"Emperors of Soul" is a 1994 five-disc box set compilation released by Motown Records, dedicated to **The Temptations** — an American vocal group and Motown artists. The set spans approximately four decades of their career, beginning with their early work as **The Distants** (e.g., the 1959 recording *"Come On"*) and concluding with material up to 1994, including four new recordings by the then-current Temptations lineup. retrieved_docs: Six Thinking Hats | Six Thinking Hats is a system designed by Edward de Bono which describes a tool for group discussion and individual thinking involving six colored hats.  "Six Thinking Hats" and the associated idea parallel thinking provide a means for groups to plan thinking processes in a detailed and cohesive way, and in doing so to think together more effectively.

I Think You Think Too Much of Me | I Think You Think Too Much of Me (stylised as i think you think too much of me) is the second EP by Irish musician EDEN, released on 19 August 2016 through his own label MCMXCV and distributed by Astralwerks.  The EP was recorded and produced in Dublin and contains seven tracks spanning approximately 26 minutes.  "i think you think too much of me" is the first of Ng's works to be released on physical media, as it was produced in two 12-inch vinyl formats, clear red and clear, in addition to Compact Cassette.

Oh, the Thinks You Can Think! | Oh, the Thinks You Can Think!  is a children's book written and illustrated by Theodor Geisel under the pen name Dr. Seuss and published by Random House on August 21, 1975.  The book is about the many amazing 'thinks' one can think and the endless possibilities and dreams that imagination can create.

Thinking and Destiny | "Thinking and Destiny" by Harold W. Percival on "The law of thought".  Its basic premise is that thinking creates karma or destiny.  The book shows how to perfect our thinking and learn how to think without creating thoughts.

Thinking outside the box | Thinking outside the box (also thinking out of the box or thinking beyond the box and, especially in Australia, thinking outside the square) is a metaphor that means to think differently, unconventionally, or from a new perspective.  This phrase often refers to novel or creative thinking.  The term is thought to derive from management consultants in the 1970s and 1980s challenging their clients to solve the "nine dots" puzzle, whose solution requires some lateral thinking.  This phrase can also be found commonly in dance, as encouragement to move creatively, beyond simple, geometric box steps and their basic variations, to literally step outside the box into more complex patterns of expression.

Speed thinking | Speed thinking is a type of thinking technique proposed by authors such as Dr Ken Hudson, Malcolm Gladwell and Edward De Bono that, according to the authors, could accelerate the pace of thinking and improve how an individual or group thinks, creates, solves and acts.
Visual thinking | Visual thinking, also called visual/spatial learning or picture thinking is the phenomenon of thinking through visual processing.

retrieved_titles: I Think You Think Too Much of Me
Oh, the Thinks You Can Think!
retrieved_docs: Six Thinking Hats | Six Thinking Hats is a system designed by Edward de Bono which describes a tool for group discussion and individual thinking involving six colored hats.  "Six Thinking Hats" and the associated idea parallel thinking provide a means for groups to plan thinking processes in a detailed and cohesive way, and in doing so to think together more effectively.

I Think You Think Too Much of Me | I Think You Think Too Much of Me (stylised as i think you think too much of me) is the second EP by Irish musician EDEN, released on 19 August 2016 through his own label MCMXCV and distributed by Astralwerks.  The EP was recorded and produced in Dublin and contains seven tracks spanning approximately 26 minutes.  "i think you think too much of me" is the first of Ng's works to be released on physical media, as it was produced in two 12-inch vinyl formats, clear red and clear, in addition to Compact Cassette.

Oh, the Thinks You Can Think! | Oh, the Thinks You Can Think!  is a children's book written and illustrated by Theodor Geisel under the pen name Dr. Seuss and published by Random House on August 21, 1975.  The book is about the many amazing 'thinks' one can think and the endless possibilities and dreams that imagination can create.

Thinking and Destiny | "Thinking and Destiny" by Harold W. Percival on "The law of thought".  Its basic premise is that thinking creates karma or destiny.  The book shows how to perfect our thinking and learn how to think without creating thoughts.

Thinking outside the box | Thinking outside the box (also thinking out of the box or thinking beyond the box and, especially in Australia, thinking outside the square) is a metaphor that means to think differently, unconventionally, or from a new perspective.  This phrase often refers to novel or creative thinking.  The term is thought to derive from management consultants in the 1970s and 1980s challenging their clients to solve the "nine dots" puzzle, whose solution requires some lateral thinking.  This phrase can also be found commonly in dance, as encouragement to move creatively, beyond simple, geometric box steps and their basic variations, to literally step outside the box into more complex patterns of expression.

Speed thinking | Speed thinking is a type of thinking technique proposed by authors such as Dr Ken Hudson, Malcolm Gladwell and Edward De Bono that, according to the authors, could accelerate the pace of thinking and improve how an individual or group thinks, creates, solves and acts.
Visual thinking | Visual thinking, also called visual/spatial learning or picture thinking is the phenomenon of thinking through visual processing.

retrieved_docs: Six Thinking Hats | Six Thinking Hats is a system designed by Edward de Bono which describes a tool for group discussion and individual thinking involving six colored hats.  "Six Thinking Hats" and the associated idea parallel thinking provide a means for groups to plan thinking processes in a detailed and cohesive way, and in doing so to think together more effectively.

I Think You Think Too Much of Me | I Think You Think Too Much of Me (stylised as i think you think too much of me) is the second EP by Irish musician EDEN, released on 19 August 2016 through his own label MCMXCV and distributed by Astralwerks.  The EP was recorded and produced in Dublin and contains seven tracks spanning approximately 26 minutes.  "i think you think too much of me" is the first of Ng's works to be released on physical media, as it was produced in two 12-inch vinyl formats, clear red and clear, in addition to Compact Cassette.

Oh, the Thinks You Can Think! | Oh, the Thinks You Can Think!  is a children's book written and illustrated by Theodor Geisel under the pen name Dr. Seuss and published by Random House on August 21, 1975.  The book is about the many amazing 'thinks' one can think and the endless possibilities and dreams that imagination can create.

Thinking and Destiny | "Thinking and Destiny" by Harold W. Percival on "The law of thought".  Its basic premise is that thinking creates karma or destiny.  The book shows how to perfect our thinking and learn how to think without creating thoughts.

Thinking outside the box | Thinking outside the box (also thinking out of the box or thinking beyond the box and, especially in Australia, thinking outside the square) is a metaphor that means to think differently, unconventionally, or from a new perspective.  This phrase often refers to novel or creative thinking.  The term is thought to derive from management consultants in the 1970s and 1980s challenging their clients to solve the "nine dots" puzzle, whose solution requires some lateral thinking.  This phrase can also be found commonly in dance, as encouragement to move creatively, beyond simple, geometric box steps and their basic variations, to literally step outside the box into more complex patterns of expression.

Speed thinking | Speed thinking is a type of thinking technique proposed by authors such as Dr Ken Hudson, Malcolm Gladwell and Edward De Bono that, according to the authors, could accelerate the pace of thinking and improve how an individual or group thinks, creates, solves and acts.
Visual thinking | Visual thinking, also called visual/spatial learning or picture thinking is the phenomenon of thinking through visual processing.

I Think You Think Too Much of Me | I Think You Think Too Much of Me (stylised as i think you think too much of me) is the second EP by Irish musician EDEN, released on 19 August 2016 through his own label MCMXCV and distributed by Astralwerks. "i think you think too much of me" is the first of Ng's works to be released on physical media, as it was produced in two 12-inch vinyl formats, clear red and clear, in addition to Compact Cassette.

This phrase often refers to novel or creative thinking.  The term is thought to derive from management consultants in the 1970s and 1980s challenging their clients to solve the "nine dots" puzzle, whose solution requires some lateral thinking.  This phrase can also be found commonly in dance, as encouragement to move creatively, beyond simple, geometric box steps and their basic variations, to literally step outside the box into more complex patterns of expression.

Visual thinking | Visual thinking, also called visual/spatial learning or picture thinking is the phenomenon of thinking through visual processing.

retrieved_docs: Six Thinking Hats | Six Thinking Hats is a system designed by Edward de Bono which describes a tool for group discussion and individual thinking involving six colored hats.  "Six Thinking Hats" and the associated idea parallel thinking provide a means for groups to plan thinking processes in a detailed and cohesive way, and in doing so to think together more effectively.

I Think You Think Too Much of Me | I Think You Think Too Much of Me (stylised as i think you think too much of me) is the second EP by Irish musician EDEN, released on 19 August 2016 through his own label MCMXCV and distributed by Astralwerks.  The EP was recorded and produced in Dublin and contains seven tracks spanning approximately 26 minutes.  "i think you think too much of me" is the first of Ng's works to be released on physical media, as it was produced in two 12-inch vinyl formats, clear red and clear, in addition to Compact Cassette.

Oh, the Thinks You Can Think! | Oh, the Thinks You Can Think!  is a children's book written and illustrated by Theodor Geisel under the pen name Dr. Seuss and published by Random House on August 21, 1975.  The book is about the many amazing 'thinks' one can think and the endless possibilities and dreams that imagination can create.

Thinking and Destiny | "Thinking and Destiny" by Harold W. Percival on "The law of thought".  Its basic premise is that thinking creates karma or destiny.  The book shows how to perfect our thinking and learn how to think without creating thoughts.

Thinking outside the box | Thinking outside the box (also thinking out of the box or thinking beyond the box and, especially in Australia, thinking outside the square) is a metaphor that means to think differently, unconventionally, or from a new perspective.  This phrase often refers to novel or creative thinking.  The term is thought to derive from management consultants in the 1970s and 1980s challenging their clients to solve the "nine dots" puzzle, whose solution requires some lateral thinking.  This phrase can also be found commonly in dance, as encouragement to move creatively, beyond simple, geometric box steps and their basic variations, to literally step outside the box into more complex patterns of expression.

Speed thinking | Speed thinking is a type of thinking technique proposed by authors such as Dr Ken Hudson, Malcolm Gladwell and Edward De Bono that, according to the authors, could accelerate the pace of thinking and improve how an individual or group thinks, creates, solves and acts.
Visual thinking | Visual thinking, also called visual/spatial learning or picture thinking is the phenomenon of thinking through visual processing.

retrieved_docs: Six Thinking Hats | Six Thinking Hats is a system designed by Edward de Bono which describes a tool for group discussion and individual thinking involving six colored hats. I Think You Think Too Much of Me | I Think You Think Too Much of Me (stylised as i think you think too much of me) is the second EP by Irish musician EDEN, released on 19 August 2016 through his own label MCMXCV and distributed by Astralwerks.  The EP was recorded and produced in Dublin and contains seven tracks spanning approximately 26 minutes.  "i think you think too much of me" is the first of Ng's works to be released on physical media, as it was produced in two 12-inch vinyl formats, clear red and clear, in addition to Compact Cassette.

Oh, the Thinks You Can Think! | Oh, the Thinks You Can Think!  is a children's book written and illustrated by Theodor Geisel under the pen name Dr. Seuss and published by Random House on August 21, 1975.  The book is about the many amazing 'thinks' one can think and the endless possibilities and dreams that imagination can create.

Thinking and Destiny | "Thinking and Destiny" by Harold W. Percival on "The law of thought".  Its basic premise is that thinking creates karma or destiny.  The book shows how to perfect our thinking and learn how to think without creating thoughts.

Thinking outside the box | Thinking outside the box (also thinking out of the box or thinking beyond the box and, especially in Australia, thinking outside the square) is a metaphor that means to think differently, unconventionally, or from a new perspective.  This phrase often refers to novel or creative thinking.  The term is thought to derive from management consultants in the 1970s and 1980s challenging their clients to solve the "nine dots" puzzle, whose solution requires some lateral thinking.  This phrase can also be found commonly in dance, as encouragement to move creatively, beyond simple, geometric box steps and their basic variations, to literally step outside the box into more complex patterns of expression.

Speed thinking | Speed thinking is a type of thinking technique proposed by authors such as Dr Ken Hudson, Malcolm Gladwell and Edward De Bono that, according to the authors, could accelerate the pace of thinking and improve how an individual or group thinks, creates, solves and acts.
Visual thinking | Visual thinking, also called visual/spatial learning or picture thinking is the phenomenon of thinking through visual processing.

retrieved_titles: I Think You Think Too Much of Me
Oh, the Thinks You Can Think!
retrieved_docs: Six Thinking Hats | Six Thinking Hats is a system designed by Edward de Bono which describes a tool for group discussion and individual thinking involving six colored hats.  "Six Thinking Hats" and the associated idea parallel thinking provide a means for groups to plan thinking processes in a detailed and cohesive way, and in doing so to think together more effectively.

I Think You Think Too Much of Me | I Think You Think Too Much of Me (stylised as i think you think too much of me) is the second EP by Irish musician EDEN, released on 19 August 2016 through his own label MCMXCV and distributed by Astralwerks.  The EP was recorded and produced in Dublin and contains seven tracks spanning approximately 26 minutes.  "i think you think too much of me" is the first of Ng's works to be released on physical media, as it was produced in two 12-inch vinyl formats, clear red and clear, in addition to Compact Cassette.

Oh, the Thinks You Can Think! | Oh, the Thinks You Can Think!  is a children's book written and illustrated by Theodor Geisel under the pen name Dr. Seuss and published by Random House on August 21, 1975.  The book is about the many amazing 'thinks' one can think and the endless possibilities and dreams that imagination can create.

Thinking and Destiny | "Thinking and Destiny" by Harold W. Percival on "The law of thought".  Its basic premise is that thinking creates karma or destiny.  The book shows how to perfect our thinking and learn how to think without creating thoughts.

Thinking outside the box | Thinking outside the box (also thinking out of the box or thinking beyond the box and, especially in Australia, thinking outside the square) is a metaphor that means to think differently, unconventionally, or from a new perspective.  This phrase often refers to novel or creative thinking.  The term is thought to derive from management consultants in the 1970s and 1980s challenging their clients to solve the "nine dots" puzzle, whose solution requires some lateral thinking.  This phrase can also be found commonly in dance, as encouragement to move creatively, beyond simple, geometric box steps and their basic variations, to literally step outside the box into more complex patterns of expression.

Speed thinking | Speed thinking is a type of thinking technique proposed by authors such as Dr Ken Hudson, Malcolm Gladwell and Edward De Bono that, according to the authors, could accelerate the pace of thinking and improve how an individual or group thinks, creates, solves and acts.
Visual thinking | Visual thinking, also called visual/spatial learning or picture thinking is the phenomenon of thinking through visual processing.

Oh, the Thinks You Can Think!
retrieved_docs: Six Thinking Hats | Six Thinking Hats is a system designed by Edward de Bono which describes a tool for group discussion and individual thinking involving six colored hats.  "Six Thinking Hats" and the associated idea parallel thinking provide a means for groups to plan thinking processes in a detailed and cohesive way, and in doing so to think together more effectively.

I Think You Think Too Much of Me | I Think You Think Too Much of Me (stylised as i think you think too much of me) is the second EP by Irish musician EDEN, released on 19 August 2016 through his own label MCMXCV and distributed by Astralwerks.  The EP was recorded and produced in Dublin and contains seven tracks spanning approximately 26 minutes.  "i think you think too much of me" is the first of Ng's works to be released on physical media, as it was produced in two 12-inch vinyl formats, clear red and clear, in addition to Compact Cassette.

Oh, the Thinks You Can Think! | Oh, the Thinks You Can Think!  is a children's book written and illustrated by Theodor Geisel under the pen name Dr. Seuss and published by Random House on August 21, 1975.  The book is about the many amazing 'thinks' one can think and the endless possibilities and dreams that imagination can create.

Thinking and Destiny | "Thinking and Destiny" by Harold W. Percival on "The law of thought".  Its basic premise is that thinking creates karma or destiny.  The book shows how to perfect our thinking and learn how to think without creating thoughts.

Thinking outside the box | Thinking outside the box (also thinking out of the box or thinking beyond the box and, especially in Australia, thinking outside the square) is a metaphor that means to think differently, unconventionally, or from a new perspective.  This phrase often refers to novel or creative thinking.  The term is thought to derive from management consultants in the 1970s and 1980s challenging their clients to solve the "nine dots" puzzle, whose solution requires some lateral thinking.  This phrase can also be found commonly in dance, as encouragement to move creatively, beyond simple, geometric box steps and their basic variations, to literally step outside the box into more complex patterns of expression.

Speed thinking | Speed thinking is a type of thinking technique proposed by authors such as Dr Ken Hudson, Malcolm Gladwell and Edward De Bono that, according to the authors, could accelerate the pace of thinking and improve how an individual or group thinks, creates, solves and acts.
Visual thinking | Visual thinking, also called visual/spatial learning or picture thinking is the phenomenon of thinking through visual processing.

retrieved_docs: Six Thinking Hats | Six Thinking Hats is a system designed by Edward de Bono which describes a tool for group discussion and individual thinking involving six colored hats.  "Six Thinking Hats" and the associated idea parallel thinking provide a means for groups to plan thinking processes in a detailed and cohesive way, and in doing so to think together more effectively.

I Think You Think Too Much of Me | I Think You Think Too Much of Me (stylised as i think you think too much of me) is the second EP by Irish musician EDEN, released on 19 August 2016 through his own label MCMXCV and distributed by Astralwerks.  The EP was recorded and produced in Dublin and contains seven tracks spanning approximately 26 minutes.  "i think you think too much of me" is the first of Ng's works to be released on physical media, as it was produced in two 12-inch vinyl formats, clear red and clear, in addition to Compact Cassette.

Oh, the Thinks You Can Think! | Oh, the Thinks You Can Think!  is a children's book written and illustrated by Theodor Geisel under the pen name Dr. Seuss and published by Random House on August 21, 1975.  The book is about the many amazing 'thinks' one can think and the endless possibilities and dreams that imagination can create.

Thinking and Destiny | "Thinking and Destiny" by Harold W. Percival on "The law of thought".  Its basic premise is that thinking creates karma or destiny.  The book shows how to perfect our thinking and learn how to think without creating thoughts.

Thinking outside the box | Thinking outside the box (also thinking out of the box or thinking beyond the box and, especially in Australia, thinking outside the square) is a metaphor that means to think differently, unconventionally, or from a new perspective.  This phrase often refers to novel or creative thinking.  The term is thought to derive from management consultants in the 1970s and 1980s challenging their clients to solve the "nine dots" puzzle, whose solution requires some lateral thinking.  This phrase can also be found commonly in dance, as encouragement to move creatively, beyond simple, geometric box steps and their basic variations, to literally step outside the box into more complex patterns of expression.

Speed thinking | Speed thinking is a type of thinking technique proposed by authors such as Dr Ken Hudson, Malcolm Gladwell and Edward De Bono that, according to the authors, could accelerate the pace of thinking and improve how an individual or group thinks, creates, solves and acts.
Visual thinking | Visual thinking, also called visual/spatial learning or picture thinking is the phenomenon of thinking through visual processing.

I Think You Think Too Much of Me | I Think You Think Too Much of Me (stylised as i think you think too much of me) is the second EP by Irish musician EDEN, released on 19 August 2016 through his own label MCMXCV and distributed by Astralwerks.  The EP was recorded and produced in Dublin and contains seven tracks spanning approximately 26 minutes.  "i think you think too much of me" is the first of Ng's works to be released on physical media, as it was produced in two 12-inch vinyl formats, clear red and clear, in addition to Compact Cassette.

Oh, the Thinks You Can Think! | Oh, the Thinks You Can Think!  is a children's book written and illustrated by Theodor Geisel under the pen name Dr. Seuss and published by Random House on August 21, 1975. Thinking and Destiny | "Thinking and Destiny" by Harold W. Percival on "The law of thought".  Its basic premise is that thinking creates karma or destiny.  The book shows how to perfect our thinking and learn how to think without creating thoughts.

Thinking outside the box | Thinking outside the box (also thinking out of the box or thinking beyond the box and, especially in Australia, thinking outside the square) is a metaphor that means to think differently, unconventionally, or from a new perspective.  This phrase often refers to novel or creative thinking.  The term is thought to derive from management consultants in the 1970s and 1980s challenging their clients to solve the "nine dots" puzzle, whose solution requires some lateral thinking. Speed thinking | Speed thinking is a type of thinking technique proposed by authors such as Dr Ken Hudson, Malcolm Gladwell and Edward De Bono that, according to the authors, could accelerate the pace of thinking and improve how an individual or group thinks, creates, solves and acts.
Visual thinking | Visual thinking, also called visual/spatial learning or picture thinking is the phenomenon of thinking through visual processing.

question: Who was born first, Theodore H. White or Nelson DeMille?
updated_prompt:
Given `question` and `summary_1`, produce a second-hop BM25 search query.

The query must be a compact keyword query, not an explanation.

Reversibility constraints:
- Preserve task-relevant named entities, titles, aliases, dates, relations, and uncertainty markers that are present in `question` or `summary_1`.
- Do not introduce new entities, aliases, facts, candidate answers, or support titles not present in the input.
- If a candidate is uncertain, preserve it as uncertain; do not treat it as confirmed.
- If multiple candidates are present, keep them as a candidate set rather than collapsing to one candidate.
- The original retrieval problem and correction direction should be recoverable from the query behavior.

Optimization-usefulness constraints:
- Do not merely restate the full question.
- Do not produce generic role-only queries when concrete names or titles are available.
- Make the query expose a useful retrieval direction: what to preserve, what to verify, and what missing relation to target.

Representation guide:
Use a reversible edit-script representation.
Imagine transforming a weak query into a stronger one:
keep essential anchors, restore missing relation words, retain uncertain candidates as a set, and delete only irrelevant verbosity.
The final query should reflect the edited behavior.

Minibatch emphasis:
Prioritize missing-support recovery.
The useful query should retrieve the page that was missing after the first hop or after a weak second-hop query.
Favor terms that identify the support page, relation evidence, and answer-bearing page.

Rewrite style:
Be conservative.
Prefer high reversibility and explicit lexical preservation.
Use enough context for BM25, but avoid long explanatory sentences.
Do not over-generalize.

Output rules:
- Output only the query.
- Use concrete names, titles, aliases, and relation terms.
- Do not output bullet points, explanations, or JSON.
- Do not invent facts.

Additional directive: Explicitly include birth dates of uncertain candidates when they are critical to the comparison.

retrieved_titles: I Think You Think Too Much of Me
Oh, the Thinks You Can Think!
retrieved_docs: Six Thinking Hats | Six Thinking Hats is a system designed by Edward de Bono which describes a tool for group discussion and individual thinking involving six colored hats.  "Six Thinking Hats" and the associated idea parallel thinking provide a means for groups to plan thinking processes in a detailed and cohesive way, and in doing so to think together more effectively.

I Think You Think Too Much of Me | I Think You Think Too Much of Me (stylised as i think you think too much of me) is the second EP by Irish musician EDEN, released on 19 August 2016 through his own label MCMXCV and distributed by Astralwerks.  The EP was recorded and produced in Dublin and contains seven tracks spanning approximately 26 minutes.  "i think you think too much of me" is the first of Ng's works to be released on physical media, as it was produced in two 12-inch vinyl formats, clear red and clear, in addition to Compact Cassette.

Oh, the Thinks You Can Think! | Oh, the Thinks You Can Think!  is a children's book written and illustrated by Theodor Geisel under the pen name Dr. Seuss and published by Random House on August 21, 1975.  The book is about the many amazing 'thinks' one can think and the endless possibilities and dreams that imagination can create.

Thinking and Destiny | "Thinking and Destiny" by Harold W. Percival on "The law of thought".  Its basic premise is that thinking creates karma or destiny.  The book shows how to perfect our thinking and learn how to think without creating thoughts.

Thinking outside the box | Thinking outside the box (also thinking out of the box or thinking beyond the box and, especially in Australia, thinking outside the square) is a metaphor that means to think differently, unconventionally, or from a new perspective.  This phrase often refers to novel or creative thinking.  The term is thought to derive from management consultants in the 1970s and 1980s challenging their clients to solve the "nine dots" puzzle, whose solution requires some lateral thinking.  This phrase can also be found commonly in dance, as encouragement to move creatively, beyond simple, geometric box steps and their basic variations, to literally step outside the box into more complex patterns of expression.

Speed thinking | Speed thinking is a type of thinking technique proposed by authors such as Dr Ken Hudson, Malcolm Gladwell and Edward De Bono that, according to the authors, could accelerate the pace of thinking and improve how an individual or group thinks, creates, solves and acts.
Visual thinking | Visual thinking, also called visual/spatial learning or picture thinking is the phenomenon of thinking through visual processing.

retrieved_docs: Six Thinking Hats | Six Thinking Hats is a system designed by Edward de Bono which describes a tool for group discussion and individual thinking involving six colored hats. I Think You Think Too Much of Me | I Think You Think Too Much of Me (stylised as i think you think too much of me) is the second EP by Irish musician EDEN, released on 19 August 2016 through his own label MCMXCV and distributed by Astralwerks.  The EP was recorded and produced in Dublin and contains seven tracks spanning approximately 26 minutes.  "i think you think too much of me" is the first of Ng's works to be released on physical media, as it was produced in two 12-inch vinyl formats, clear red and clear, in addition to Compact Cassette.

Oh, the Thinks You Can Think! | Oh, the Thinks You Can Think!  is a children's book written and illustrated by Theodor Geisel under the pen name Dr. Seuss and published by Random House on August 21, 1975.  The book is about the many amazing 'thinks' one can think and the endless possibilities and dreams that imagination can create.

Thinking and Destiny | "Thinking and Destiny" by Harold W. Percival on "The law of thought".  Its basic premise is that thinking creates karma or destiny.  The book shows how to perfect our thinking and learn how to think without creating thoughts.

Thinking outside the box | Thinking outside the box (also thinking out of the box or thinking beyond the box and, especially in Australia, thinking outside the square) is a metaphor that means to think differently, unconventionally, or from a new perspective.  This phrase often refers to novel or creative thinking.  The term is thought to derive from management consultants in the 1970s and 1980s challenging their clients to solve the "nine dots" puzzle, whose solution requires some lateral thinking.  This phrase can also be found commonly in dance, as encouragement to move creatively, beyond simple, geometric box steps and their basic variations, to literally step outside the box into more complex patterns of expression.

Visual thinking | Visual thinking, also called visual/spatial learning or picture thinking is the phenomenon of thinking through visual processing.

updated_prompt:
Given `question` and `summary_1`, produce a second-hop BM25 search query.

The query must be a compact keyword query, not an explanation.

Reversibility constraints:
- Preserve task-relevant named entities, titles, aliases, dates, relations, and uncertainty markers that are present in `question` or `summary_1`.
- Do not introduce new entities, aliases, facts, candidate answers, or support titles not present in the input.
- If a candidate is uncertain, preserve it as uncertain; do not treat it as confirmed.
- If multiple candidates are present, keep them as a candidate set rather than collapsing to one candidate.
- The original retrieval problem and correction direction should be recoverable from the query behavior.

Optimization-usefulness constraints:
- Do not merely restate the full question.
- Do not produce generic role-only queries when concrete names or titles are available.
- Make the query expose a useful retrieval direction: what to preserve, what to verify, and what missing relation to target.

Representation guide:
Use a reversible edit-script representation.
Imagine transforming a weak query into a stronger one:
keep essential anchors, restore missing relation words, retain uncertain candidates as a set, and delete only irrelevant verbosity.
The final query should reflect the edited behavior.

Minibatch emphasis:
Prioritize missing-support recovery.
The useful query should retrieve the page that was missing after the first hop or after a weak second-hop query.
Favor terms that identify the support page, relation evidence, and answer-bearing page.

Rewrite style:
Be conservative.
Prefer high reversibility and explicit lexical preservation.
Use enough context for BM25, but avoid long explanatory sentences.
Do not over-generalize.

Output rules:
- Output only the query.
- Use concrete names, titles, aliases, and relation terms.
- Do not output bullet points, explanations, or JSON.
- Do not invent facts.

Focus on Emmy award specifics: include "Emmy category" and "Emmy year" when available, and prioritize answer-bearing entity pages over event/mission/location pages.

The updated prompt adds explicit emphasis on Emmy category/year and prioritizes entity pages over media titles, addressing the pattern of missing support for Emmy award specifics.

retrieved_titles: I Think You Think Too Much of Me

retrieved_docs: Six Thinking Hats | Six Thinking Hats is a system designed by Edward de Bono which describes a tool for group discussion and individual thinking involving six colored hats.  "Six Thinking Hats" and the associated idea parallel thinking provide a means for groups to plan thinking processes in a detailed and cohesive way, and in doing so to think together more effectively.

I Think You Think Too Much of Me | I Think You Think Too Much of Me (stylised as i think you think too much of me) is the second EP by Irish musician EDEN, released on 19 August 2016 through his own label MCMXCV and distributed by Astralwerks.  The EP was recorded and produced in Dublin and contains seven tracks spanning approximately 26 minutes.  "i think you think too much of me" is the first of Ng's works to be released on physical media, as it was produced in two 12-inch vinyl formats, clear red and clear, in addition to Compact Cassette.

Oh, the Thinks You Can Think! | Oh, the Thinks You Can Think!  is a children's book written and illustrated by Theodor Geisel under the pen name Dr. Seuss and published by Random House on August 21, 1975.  The book is about the many amazing 'thinks' one can think and the endless possibilities and dreams that imagination can create.

Thinking and Destiny | "Thinking and Destiny" by Harold W. Percival on "The law of thought".  Its basic premise is that thinking creates karma or destiny.  The book shows how to perfect our thinking and learn how to think without creating thoughts.

Thinking outside the box | Thinking outside the box (also thinking out of the box or thinking beyond the box and, especially in Australia, thinking outside the square) is a metaphor that means to think differently, unconventionally, or from a new perspective.  This phrase often refers to novel or creative thinking.  The term is thought to derive from management consultants in the 1970s and 1980s challenging their clients to solve the "nine dots" puzzle, whose solution requires some lateral thinking.  This phrase can also be found commonly in dance, as encouragement to move creatively, beyond simple, geometric box steps and their basic variations, to literally step outside the box into more complex patterns of expression.

Speed thinking | Speed thinking is a type of thinking technique proposed by authors such as Dr Ken Hudson, Malcolm Gladwell and Edward De Bono that, according to the authors, could accelerate the pace of thinking and improve how an individual or group thinks, creates, solves and acts.
Visual thinking | Visual thinking, also called visual/spatial learning or picture thinking is the phenomenon of thinking through visual processing.

retrieved_docs: Six Thinking Hats | Six Thinking Hats is a system designed by Edward de Bono which describes a tool for group discussion and individual thinking involving six colored hats.  "Six Thinking Hats" and the associated idea parallel thinking provide a means for groups to plan thinking processes in a detailed and cohesive way, and in doing so to think together more effectively.

I Think You Think Too Much of Me | I Think You Think Too Much of Me (stylised as i think you think too much of me) is the second EP by Irish musician EDEN, released on 19 August 2016 through his own label MCMXCV and distributed by Astralwerks.  The EP was recorded and produced in Dublin and contains seven tracks spanning approximately 26 minutes.  "i think you think too much of me" is the first of Ng's works to be released on physical media, as it was produced in two 12-inch vinyl formats, clear red and clear, in addition to Compact Cassette.

Oh, the Thinks You Can Think! | Oh, the Thinks You Can Think!  is a children's book written and illustrated by Theodor Geisel under the pen name Dr. Seuss and published by Random House on August 21, 1975.  The book is about the many amazing 'thinks' one can think and the endless possibilities and dreams that imagination can create.

Thinking and Destiny | "Thinking and Destiny" by Harold W. Percival on "The law of thought".  Its basic premise is that thinking creates karma or destiny.  The book shows how to perfect our thinking and learn how to think without creating thoughts.

Thinking outside the box | Thinking outside the box (also thinking out of the box or thinking beyond the box and, especially in Australia, thinking outside the square) is a metaphor that means to think differently, unconventionally, or from a new perspective.  This phrase often refers to novel or creative thinking.  The term is thought to derive from management consultants in the 1970s and 1980s challenging their clients to solve the "nine dots" puzzle, whose solution requires some lateral thinking.  This phrase can also be found commonly in dance, as encouragement to move creatively, beyond simple, geometric box steps and their basic variations, to literally step outside the box into more complex patterns of expression.

Speed thinking | Speed thinking is a type of thinking technique proposed by authors such as Dr Ken Hudson, Malcolm Gladwell and Edward De Bono that, according to the authors, could accelerate the pace of thinking and improve how an individual or group thinks, creates, solves and acts.
Visual thinking | Visual thinking, also called visual/spatial learning or picture thinking is the phenomenon of thinking through visual processing.

retrieved_docs: Six Thinking Hats | Six Thinking Hats is a system designed by Edward de Bono which describes a tool for group discussion and individual thinking involving six colored hats.  "Six Thinking Hats" and the associated idea parallel thinking provide a means for groups to plan thinking processes in a detailed and cohesive way, and in doing so to think together more effectively.

I Think You Think Too Much of Me | I Think You Think Too Much of Me (stylised as i think you think too much of me) is the second EP by Irish musician EDEN, released on 19 August 2016 through his own label MCMXCV and distributed by Astralwerks.  The EP was recorded and produced in Dublin and contains seven tracks spanning approximately 26 minutes.  "i think you think too much of me" is the first of Ng's works to be released on physical media, as it was produced in two 12-inch vinyl formats, clear red and clear, in addition to Compact Cassette.

Oh, the Thinks You Can Think! | Oh, the Thinks You Can Think!  is a children's book written and illustrated by Theodor Geisel under the pen name Dr. Seuss and published by Random House on August 21, 1975.  The book is about the many amazing 'thinks' one can think and the endless possibilities and dreams that imagination can create.

Thinking and Destiny | "Thinking and Destiny" by Harold W. Percival on "The law of thought".  Its basic premise is that thinking creates karma or destiny.  The book shows how to perfect our thinking and learn how to think without creating thoughts.

Thinking outside the box | Thinking outside the box (also thinking out of the box or thinking beyond the box and, especially in Australia, thinking outside the square) is a metaphor that means to think differently, unconventionally, or from a new perspective.  This phrase often refers to novel or creative thinking.  The term is thought to derive from management consultants in the 1970s and 1980s challenging their clients to solve the "nine dots" puzzle, whose solution requires some lateral thinking.  This phrase can also be found commonly in dance, as encouragement to move creatively, beyond simple, geometric box steps and their basic variations, to literally step outside the box into more complex patterns of expression.

Speed thinking | Speed thinking is a type of thinking technique proposed by authors such as Dr Ken Hudson, Malcolm Gladwell and Edward De Bono that, according to the authors, could accelerate the pace of thinking and improve how an individual or group thinks, creates, solves and acts.
Visual thinking | Visual thinking, also called visual/spatial learning or picture thinking is the phenomenon of thinking through visual processing.

**summary**  
James Caudy, a Dutch-born American frontiersman and landowner in the Cacapon River valley (near present-day Capon Bridge, Hampshire County, Colony of Virginia—now West Virginia), hosted George Washington twice: during Washington's 1748 surveying expedition and again on Washington's 1750 return to the Cacapon River valley. George Washington, who later became the first President of the United States and was widely nicknamed "Father of His Country," was a surveyor in Virginia during the 1740s before becoming a military leader. The event connects Caudy's role as a local settler with Washington's early career, highlighting their historical interaction in the region.  

**Key Elements Included:**  
- **Entity**: James Caudy and George Washington.  
- **Attributes**: Caudy's role as a frontiersman and landowner; Washington's title as "Father of His Country" and his status as the first U.S. President.  
- **Dates**: 1748 (expedition) and 1750 (return).  
- **Locations**: Cacapon River valley, Capon Bridge, Hampshire County (West Virginia), and its historical context as part of the Colony of Virginia.  
- **Contextual Connections**: The Cacapon River valley's significance as a site of early American settlement and Washington's surveying work.  

**Note**: The `passages` provided contain no relevant information about James Caudy or George Washington, so the summary is derived solely from the `context`.

**answer**  
James Caudy hosted George Washington, who became the "Father of His Country" and later served as the first President of the United States. I Think You Think Too Much of Me | I Think You Think Too Much of Me (stylised as i think you think too much of me) is the second EP by Irish musician EDEN, released on 19 August 2016 through his own label MCMXCV and distributed by Astralwerks.  The EP was recorded and produced in Dublin and contains seven tracks spanning approximately 26 minutes.  "i think you think too much of me" is the first of Ng's works to be released on physical media, as it was produced in two 12-inch vinyl formats, clear red and clear, in addition to Compact Cassette.

Oh, the Thinks You Can Think! | Oh, the Thinks You Can Think!  is a children's book written and illustrated by Theodor Geisel under the pen name Dr. Seuss and published by Random House on August 21, 1975. This phrase often refers to novel or creative thinking.  The term is thought to derive from management consultants in the 1970s and 1980s challenging their clients to solve the "nine dots" puzzle, whose solution requires some lateral thinking. Visual thinking | Visual thinking, also called visual/spatial learning or picture thinking is the phenomenon of thinking through visual processing.

**summary**:  
James Caudy, a Dutch-born American frontiersman and landowner in the Cacapon River valley (near present-day Capon Bridge, Hampshire County, Colony of Virginia—now West Virginia), hosted George Washington twice: during Washington's 1748 surveying expedition and again on his 1750 return to the Cacapon River valley. George Washington, later the first President of the United States and widely nicknamed "Father of His Country," was a surveyor in the 1740s before becoming a military leader and president. The Cacapon River valley, where these interactions occurred, is also the namesake of "Caudy's Castle," a rock pinnacle in the same county.  

**Key Elements Included**:  
- **Entity**: George Washington (hosted by James Caudy) and his designation as "Father of His Country."  
- **Dates**: 1748 (surveying expedition), 1750 (return to the Cacapon River valley).  
- **Locations**: Cacapon River valley, Capon Bridge, Hampshire County (West Virginia), and the landmark "Caudy's Castle."  
- **Contextual Connections**: Washington's transition from surveyor to U.S. President, and the historical significance of the Cacapon River valley.  

**Note**: The `passages` provided are unrelated to the question and do not contribute to the summary. The summary prioritizes the `context` as the sole source of relevant information.

**answer**:  
James Caudy hosted George Washington, who became the "Father of His Country" and later the first President of the United States. This occurred during Washington's 1748 surveying expedition and again in 1750 when he returned to the Cacapon River valley, where Caudy was a prominent landowner. The events took place in the Cacapon River valley, near present-day Capon Bridge in Hampshire County, West Virginia. The location is also associated with the historical landmark "Caudy's Castle."

retrieved_docs: Six Thinking Hats | Six Thinking Hats is a system designed by Edward de Bono which describes a tool for group discussion and individual thinking involving six colored hats.  "Six Thinking Hats" and the associated idea parallel thinking provide a means for groups to plan thinking processes in a detailed and cohesive way, and in doing so to think together more effectively.

I Think You Think Too Much of Me | I Think You Think Too Much of Me (stylised as i think you think too much of me) is the second EP by Irish musician EDEN, released on 19 August 2016 through his own label MCMXCV and distributed by Astralwerks.  The EP was recorded and produced in Dublin and contains seven tracks spanning approximately 26 minutes.  "i think you think too much of me" is the first of Ng's works to be released on physical media, as it was produced in two 12-inch vinyl formats, clear red and clear, in addition to Compact Cassette.

Oh, the Thinks You Can Think! | Oh, the Thinks You Can Think!  is a children's book written and illustrated by Theodor Geisel under the pen name Dr. Seuss and published by Random House on August 21, 1975.  The book is about the many amazing 'thinks' one can think and the endless possibilities and dreams that imagination can create.

Thinking and Destiny | "Thinking and Destiny" by Harold W. Percival on "The law of thought".  Its basic premise is that thinking creates karma or destiny.  The book shows how to perfect our thinking and learn how to think without creating thoughts.

Thinking outside the box | Thinking outside the box (also thinking out of the box or thinking beyond the box and, especially in Australia, thinking outside the square) is a metaphor that means to think differently, unconventionally, or from a new perspective.  This phrase often refers to novel or creative thinking.  The term is thought to derive from management consultants in the 1970s and 1980s challenging their clients to solve the "nine dots" puzzle, whose solution requires some lateral thinking.  This phrase can also be found commonly in dance, as encouragement to move creatively, beyond simple, geometric box steps and their basic variations, to literally step outside the box into more complex patterns of expression.

Speed thinking | Speed thinking is a type of thinking technique proposed by authors such as Dr Ken Hudson, Malcolm Gladwell and Edward De Bono that, according to the authors, could accelerate the pace of thinking and improve how an individual or group thinks, creates, solves and acts.
Visual thinking | Visual thinking, also called visual/spatial learning or picture thinking is the phenomenon of thinking through visual processing.

retrieved_docs: Six Thinking Hats | Six Thinking Hats is a system designed by Edward de Bono which describes a tool for group discussion and individual thinking involving six colored hats.  "Six Thinking Hats" and the associated idea parallel thinking provide a means for groups to plan thinking processes in a detailed and cohesive way, and in doing so to think together more effectively.

I Think You Think Too Much of Me | I Think You Think Too Much of Me (stylised as i think you think too much of me) is the second EP by Irish musician EDEN, released on 19 August 2016 through his own label MCMXCV and distributed by Astralwerks.  The EP was recorded and produced in Dublin and contains seven tracks spanning approximately 26 minutes.  "i think you think too much of me" is the first of Ng's works to be released on physical media, as it was produced in two 12-inch vinyl formats, clear red and clear, in addition to Compact Cassette.

Oh, the Thinks You Can Think! | Oh, the Thinks You Can Think!  is a children's book written and illustrated by Theodor Geisel under the pen name Dr. Seuss and published by Random House on August 21, 1975.  The book is about the many amazing 'thinks' one can think and the endless possibilities and dreams that imagination can create.

Thinking and Destiny | "Thinking and Destiny" by Harold W. Percival on "The law of thought".  Its basic premise is that thinking creates karma or destiny.  The book shows how to perfect our thinking and learn how to think without creating thoughts.

Thinking outside the box | Thinking outside the box (also thinking out of the box or thinking beyond the box and, especially in Australia, thinking outside the square) is a metaphor that means to think differently, unconventionally, or from a new perspective.  This phrase often refers to novel or creative thinking.  The term is thought to derive from management consultants in the 1970s and 1980s challenging their clients to solve the "nine dots" puzzle, whose solution requires some lateral thinking.  This phrase can also be found commonly in dance, as encouragement to move creatively, beyond simple, geometric box steps and their basic variations, to literally step outside the box into more complex patterns of expression.

Speed thinking | Speed thinking is a type of thinking technique proposed by authors such as Dr Ken Hudson, Malcolm Gladwell and Edward De Bono that, according to the authors, could accelerate the pace of thinking and improve how an individual or group thinks, creates, solves and acts.
Visual thinking | Visual thinking, also called visual/spatial learning or picture thinking is the phenomenon of thinking through visual processing.

retrieved_docs: Six Thinking Hats | Six Thinking Hats is a system designed by Edward de Bono which describes a tool for group discussion and individual thinking involving six colored hats.  "Six Thinking Hats" and the associated idea parallel thinking provide a means for groups to plan thinking processes in a detailed and cohesive way, and in doing so to think together more effectively.

I Think You Think Too Much of Me | I Think You Think Too Much of Me (stylised as i think you think too much of me) is the second EP by Irish musician EDEN, released on 19 August 2016 through his own label MCMXCV and distributed by Astralwerks.  The EP was recorded and produced in Dublin and contains seven tracks spanning approximately 26 minutes.  "i think you think too much of me" is the first of Ng's works to be released on physical media, as it was produced in two 12-inch vinyl formats, clear red and clear, in addition to Compact Cassette.

Oh, the Thinks You Can Think! | Oh, the Thinks You Can Think!  is a children's book written and illustrated by Theodor Geisel under the pen name Dr. Seuss and published by Random House on August 21, 1975.  The book is about the many amazing 'thinks' one can think and the endless possibilities and dreams that imagination can create.

Thinking and Destiny | "Thinking and Destiny" by Harold W. Percival on "The law of thought".  Its basic premise is that thinking creates karma or destiny.  The book shows how to perfect our thinking and learn how to think without creating thoughts.

Thinking outside the box | Thinking outside the box (also thinking out of the box or thinking beyond the box and, especially in Australia, thinking outside the square) is a metaphor that means to think differently, unconventionally, or from a new perspective.  This phrase often refers to novel or creative thinking.  The term is thought to derive from management consultants in the 1970s and 1980s challenging their clients to solve the "nine dots" puzzle, whose solution requires some lateral thinking.  This phrase can also be found commonly in dance, as encouragement to move creatively, beyond simple, geometric box steps and their basic variations, to literally step outside the box into more complex patterns of expression.

Speed thinking | Speed thinking is a type of thinking technique proposed by authors such as Dr Ken Hudson, Malcolm Gladwell and Edward De Bono that, according to the authors, could accelerate the pace of thinking and improve how an individual or group thinks, creates, solves and acts.
Visual thinking | Visual thinking, also called visual/spatial learning or picture thinking is the phenomenon of thinking through visual processing.

I Think You Think Too Much of Me | I Think You Think Too Much of Me (stylised as i think you think too much of me) is the second EP by Irish musician EDEN, released on 19 August 2016 through his own label MCMXCV and distributed by Astralwerks.  The EP was recorded and produced in Dublin and contains seven tracks spanning approximately 26 minutes.  "i think you think too much of me" is the first of Ng's works to be released on physical media, as it was produced in two 12-inch vinyl formats, clear red and clear, in addition to Compact Cassette.

Oh, the Thinks You Can Think! | Oh, the Thinks You Can Think!  is a children's book written and illustrated by Theodor Geisel under the pen name Dr. Seuss and published by Random House on August 21, 1975. Thinking and Destiny | "Thinking and Destiny" by Harold W. Percival on "The law of thought".  Its basic premise is that thinking creates karma or destiny.  The book shows how to perfect our thinking and learn how to think without creating thoughts.

Thinking outside the box | Thinking outside the box (also thinking out of the box or thinking beyond the box and, especially in Australia, thinking outside the square) is a metaphor that means to think differently, unconventionally, or from a new perspective.  This phrase often refers to novel or creative thinking.  The term is thought to derive from management consultants in the 1970s and 1980s challenging their clients to solve the "nine dots" puzzle, whose solution requires some lateral thinking.  This phrase can also be found commonly in dance, as encouragement to move creatively, beyond simple, geometric box steps and their basic variations, to literally step outside the box into more complex patterns of expression.

Speed thinking | Speed thinking is a type of thinking technique proposed by authors such as Dr Ken Hudson, Malcolm Gladwell and Edward De Bono that, according to the authors, could accelerate the pace of thinking and improve how an individual or group thinks, creates, solves and acts.
Visual thinking | Visual thinking, also called visual/spatial learning or picture thinking is the phenomenon of thinking through visual processing.

This phrase often refers to novel or creative thinking.  The term is thought to derive from management consultants in the 1970s and 1980s challenging their clients to solve the "nine dots" puzzle, whose solution requires some lateral thinking. Visual thinking | Visual thinking, also called visual/spatial learning or picture thinking is the phenomenon of thinking through visual processing.

I Think You Think Too Much of Me | I Think You Think Too Much of Me (stylised as i think you think too much of me) is the second EP by Irish musician EDEN, released on 19 August 2016 through his own label MCMXCV and distributed by Astralwerks. "i think you think too much of me" is the first of Ng's works to be released on physical media, as it was produced in two 12-inch vinyl formats, clear red and clear, in addition to Compact Cassette.

Oh, the Thinks You Can Think! | Oh, the Thinks You Can Think!  is a children's book written and illustrated by Theodor Geisel under the pen name Dr. Seuss and published by Random House on August 21, 1975. Thinking and Destiny | "Thinking and Destiny" by Harold W. Percival on "The law of thought".  Its basic premise is that thinking creates karma or destiny.  The book shows how to perfect our thinking and learn how to think without creating thoughts.

This phrase often refers to novel or creative thinking.  The term is thought to derive from management consultants in the 1970s and 1980s challenging their clients to solve the "nine dots" puzzle, whose solution requires some lateral thinking. Visual thinking | Visual thinking, also called visual/spatial learning or picture thinking is the phenomenon of thinking through visual processing.

retrieved_docs: Six Thinking Hats | Six Thinking Hats is a system designed by Edward de Bono which describes a tool for group discussion and individual thinking involving six colored hats.  "Six Thinking Hats" and the associated idea parallel thinking provide a means for groups to plan thinking processes in a detailed and cohesive way, and in doing so to think together more effectively.

I Think You Think Too Much of Me | I Think You Think Too Much of Me (stylised as i think you think too much of me) is the second EP by Irish musician EDEN, released on 19 August 2016 through his own label MCMXCV and distributed by Astralwerks.  The EP was recorded and produced in Dublin and contains seven tracks spanning approximately 26 minutes.  "i think you think too much of me" is the first of Ng's works to be released on physical media, as it was produced in two 12-inch vinyl formats, clear red and clear, in addition to Compact Cassette.

Oh, the Thinks You Can Think! | Oh, the Thinks You Can Think!  is a children's book written and illustrated by Theodor Geisel under the pen name Dr. Seuss and published by Random House on August 21, 1975.  The book is about the many amazing 'thinks' one can think and the endless possibilities and dreams that imagination can create.

Thinking and Destiny | "Thinking and Destiny" by Harold W. Percival on "The law of thought".  Its basic premise is that thinking creates karma or destiny.  The book shows how to perfect our thinking and learn how to think without creating thoughts.

Thinking outside the box | Thinking outside the box (also thinking out of the box or thinking beyond the box and, especially in Australia, thinking outside the square) is a metaphor that means to think differently, unconventionally, or from a new perspective.  This phrase often refers to novel or creative thinking.  The term is thought to derive from management consultants in the 1970s and 1980s challenging their clients to solve the "nine dots" puzzle, whose solution requires some lateral thinking.  This phrase can also be found commonly in dance, as encouragement to move creatively, beyond simple, geometric box steps and their basic variations, to literally step outside the box into more complex patterns of expression.

Speed thinking | Speed thinking is a type of thinking technique proposed by authors such as Dr Ken Hudson, Malcolm Gladwell and Edward De Bono that, according to the authors, could accelerate the pace of thinking and improve how an individual or group thinks, creates, solves and acts.
Visual thinking | Visual thinking, also called visual/spatial learning or picture thinking is the phenomenon of thinking through visual processing.

retrieved_docs: Six Thinking Hats | Six Thinking Hats is a system designed by Edward de Bono which describes a tool for group discussion and individual thinking involving six colored hats.  "Six Thinking Hats" and the associated idea parallel thinking provide a means for groups to plan thinking processes in a detailed and cohesive way, and in doing so to think together more effectively.

I Think You Think Too Much of Me | I Think You Think Too Much of Me (stylised as i think you think too much of me) is the second EP by Irish musician EDEN, released on 19 August 2016 through his own label MCMXCV and distributed by Astralwerks.  The EP was recorded and produced in Dublin and contains seven tracks spanning approximately 26 minutes.  "i think you think too much of me" is the first of Ng's works to be released on physical media, as it was produced in two 12-inch vinyl formats, clear red and clear, in addition to Compact Cassette.

Oh, the Thinks You Can Think! | Oh, the Thinks You Can Think!  is a children's book written and illustrated by Theodor Geisel under the pen name Dr. Seuss and published by Random House on August 21, 1975.  The book is about the many amazing 'thinks' one can think and the endless possibilities and dreams that imagination can create.

Thinking and Destiny | "Thinking and Destiny" by Harold W. Percival on "The law of thought".  Its basic premise is that thinking creates karma or destiny.  The book shows how to perfect our thinking and learn how to think without creating thoughts.

Thinking outside the box | Thinking outside the box (also thinking out of the box or thinking beyond the box and, especially in Australia, thinking outside the square) is a metaphor that means to think differently, unconventionally, or from a new perspective.  This phrase often refers to novel or creative thinking.  The term is thought to derive from management consultants in the 1970s and 1980s challenging their clients to solve the "nine dots" puzzle, whose solution requires some lateral thinking.  This phrase can also be found commonly in dance, as encouragement to move creatively, beyond simple, geometric box steps and their basic variations, to literally step outside the box into more complex patterns of expression.

Speed thinking | Speed thinking is a type of thinking technique proposed by authors such as Dr Ken Hudson, Malcolm Gladwell and Edward De Bono that, according to the authors, could accelerate the pace of thinking and improve how an individual or group thinks, creates, solves and acts.
Visual thinking | Visual thinking, also called visual/spatial learning or picture thinking is the phenomenon of thinking through visual processing.

retrieved_docs: Six Thinking Hats | Six Thinking Hats is a system designed by Edward de Bono which describes a tool for group discussion and individual thinking involving six colored hats.  "Six Thinking Hats" and the associated idea parallel thinking provide a means for groups to plan thinking processes in a detailed and cohesive way, and in doing so to think together more effectively.

I Think You Think Too Much of Me | I Think You Think Too Much of Me (stylised as i think you think too much of me) is the second EP by Irish musician EDEN, released on 19 August 2016 through his own label MCMXCV and distributed by Astralwerks.  The EP was recorded and produced in Dublin and contains seven tracks spanning approximately 26 minutes.  "i think you think too much of me" is the first of Ng's works to be released on physical media, as it was produced in two 12-inch vinyl formats, clear red and clear, in addition to Compact Cassette.

Oh, the Thinks You Can Think! | Oh, the Thinks You Can Think!  is a children's book written and illustrated by Theodor Geisel under the pen name Dr. Seuss and published by Random House on August 21, 1975.  The book is about the many amazing 'thinks' one can think and the endless possibilities and dreams that imagination can create.

Thinking and Destiny | "Thinking and Destiny" by Harold W. Percival on "The law of thought".  Its basic premise is that thinking creates karma or destiny.  The book shows how to perfect our thinking and learn how to think without creating thoughts.

Thinking outside the box | Thinking outside the box (also thinking out of the box or thinking beyond the box and, especially in Australia, thinking outside the square) is a metaphor that means to think differently, unconventionally, or from a new perspective.  This phrase often refers to novel or creative thinking.  The term is thought to derive from management consultants in the 1970s and 1980s challenging their clients to solve the "nine dots" puzzle, whose solution requires some lateral thinking.  This phrase can also be found commonly in dance, as encouragement to move creatively, beyond simple, geometric box steps and their basic variations, to literally step outside the box into more complex patterns of expression.

Speed thinking | Speed thinking is a type of thinking technique proposed by authors such as Dr Ken Hudson, Malcolm Gladwell and Edward De Bono that, according to the authors, could accelerate the pace of thinking and improve how an individual or group thinks, creates, solves and acts.
Visual thinking | Visual thinking, also called visual/spatial learning or picture thinking is the phenomenon of thinking through visual processing.

retrieved_docs: Six Thinking Hats | Six Thinking Hats is a system designed by Edward de Bono which describes a tool for group discussion and individual thinking involving six colored hats.  "Six Thinking Hats" and the associated idea parallel thinking provide a means for groups to plan thinking processes in a detailed and cohesive way, and in doing so to think together more effectively.

I Think You Think Too Much of Me | I Think You Think Too Much of Me (stylised as i think you think too much of me) is the second EP by Irish musician EDEN, released on 19 August 2016 through his own label MCMXCV and distributed by Astralwerks.  The EP was recorded and produced in Dublin and contains seven tracks spanning approximately 26 minutes.  "i think you think too much of me" is the first of Ng's works to be released on physical media, as it was produced in two 12-inch vinyl formats, clear red and clear, in addition to Compact Cassette.

Oh, the Thinks You Can Think! | Oh, the Thinks You Can Think!  is a children's book written and illustrated by Theodor Geisel under the pen name Dr. Seuss and published by Random House on August 21, 1975.  The book is about the many amazing 'thinks' one can think and the endless possibilities and dreams that imagination can create.

Thinking and Destiny | "Thinking and Destiny" by Harold W. Percival on "The law of thought".  Its basic premise is that thinking creates karma or destiny.  The book shows how to perfect our thinking and learn how to think without creating thoughts.

Thinking outside the box | Thinking outside the box (also thinking out of the box or thinking beyond the box and, especially in Australia, thinking outside the square) is a metaphor that means to think differently, unconventionally, or from a new perspective.  This phrase often refers to novel or creative thinking.  The term is thought to derive from management consultants in the 1970s and 1980s challenging their clients to solve the "nine dots" puzzle, whose solution requires some lateral thinking.  This phrase can also be found commonly in dance, as encouragement to move creatively, beyond simple, geometric box steps and their basic variations, to literally step outside the box into more complex patterns of expression.

Speed thinking | Speed thinking is a type of thinking technique proposed by authors such as Dr Ken Hudson, Malcolm Gladwell and Edward De Bono that, according to the authors, could accelerate the pace of thinking and improve how an individual or group thinks, creates, solves and acts.
Visual thinking | Visual thinking, also called visual/spatial learning or picture thinking is the phenomenon of thinking through visual processing.
